samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 8-bit multiplier using pipelining processing.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal for multiplication.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand input.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier input.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal for multiplication.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Output product.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="WIDTH">8</param>
        <param id="WIDTH_X2">16</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg name="mul_en_out_reg" width="[1:0]">
            <description>Register to store the multiplication enable signal.</description>
        </reg>
        <reg name="mul_a_reg" width="[7:0]">
            <description>Register to store the multiplicand input.</description>
        </reg>
        <reg name="mul_b_reg" width="[7:0]">
            <description>Register to store the multiplier input.</description>
        </reg>
        <reg name="sum" width="[15:0]">
            <description>Register to store the partial sums.</description>
        </reg>
        <reg name="mul_out_reg" width="[15:0]">
            <description>Register to store the final output product.</description>
        </reg>
        <wire name="temp" width="[15:0]">
            <description>Wire to store the temporary partial products.</description>
        </wire>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <task>Implement an unsigned 8-bit multiplier using pipelining processing.</task>
        <description>
            The implementation consists of three stages: 
            1. Input control: The module includes an input enable signal (mul_en_in) that controls the multiplication operation. 
            On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk), the module samples the input enable signal and stores it in a register (mul_en_out_reg). 
            The output enable signal (mul_en_out) is derived from the most significant bit of mul_en_out_reg, indicating whether the multiplication operation is enabled.
            2. Input registers: The module includes registers (mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg) to store the input multiplicand (mul_a) and multiplier (mul_b), respectively. 
            On the positive edge of the clock signal, the module samples the input values and stores them in the corresponding registers. 
            The registers are only updated when the input enable signal is active.
            3. Partial product generation: The module generates partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. 
            It uses conditional assignments to select the appropriate bits from the multiplicand based on the multiplier bits. 
            The partial products are stored in a set of wires (temp) with each wire representing a different bit position.
            4. Partial sum calculation: The module performs addition operations on the partial products to calculate partial sums. 
            It uses a set of registers (sum) to store the intermediate sum values. 
            Each register corresponds to a group of partial products and is updated on the positive edge of the clock signal.
            5. Final product calculation: The module sums up all the partial sums to obtain the final product. 
            It uses a register (mul_out_reg) to store the accumulated sum. 
            On the positive edge of the clock signal, the register is updated with the sum of all partial sums.
            6. Output assignment: The module assigns the output product value (mul_out) based on the output enable signal (mul_en_out) and the value in mul_out_reg. 
            If the output enable signal is active, indicating a valid product, the value in mul_out_reg is assigned to mul_out. 
            Otherwise, mul_out is set to 0.
        </description>
    </implementation>
</module>
